features.name: Mechanical Handle Locking
features.rationale: Delivery mechanism for Commodity FP itself. Safety (8): direct deterrence of unauthorized dock exit use. Reliability (7): fully passive, no power dependency. Score limited because it contributes nothing to audit automation.

features.name: Audible Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Universal in competing products. Safety (9): primary incident-response trigger. Reliability (6) and Confidence (6) discounted: 95 dB alarm may be masked by 85-95 dB DC ambient noise from diesel forklifts.

features.name: Single-Motion Emergency Release
features.rationale: Non-negotiable regulatory compliance. Highest among must_be features. Skill (8): zero training required from high-turnover DC workforce. Confidence (8): regulatory certification provides hard assurance.

features.name: Key Override Bypass
features.rationale: Expected by all security managers. Lowest score — contributes nothing to top-5 opportunity needs. Pain (6): eliminates operational nuisance of alarm-triggering authorized access.

features.name: Battery-Powered Operation
features.rationale: Highest-scoring technology feature. DC exits are far from electrical infrastructure. Cost (8), Skill (8), Pain (8): enables no-electrician retrofit. Safety (5) and Reliability (5) suppressed by C4/N09 (battery depletion = undetected events).

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning
features.rationale: Absent in mechanical-only and wired alternatives. Stress (7) and Pain (7): breaks the false-alarm response cycle from dock workers brushing handles. Safety (3): pre-alarm could lull genuine intruder into abort.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of monitoring blind spots from 868 MHz signal attenuation through steel racking and corrugated metal cladding in DC facilities exceeding 5,000 m2
Minimize the effort required to plan battery replacement schedules when actual IoT module power consumption under high door-activation frequency is unpublished
Minimize the likelihood of guard misalignment from surface-mounting on non-standard or worn EN 179 lever handle geometries
Minimize the integration effort to translate EasyWave 868 MHz event messages into standard building automation formats (BACnet, Modbus, OPC-UA)
Minimize the likelihood of repeated receiver-to-gateway communication failures from RF interference from mobile warehouse equipment on adjacent frequency bands
